agreements.convergence: skeptic-f3, test-strategy-auditor-f1, test-strategy-auditor-f2, aegis-fit-auditor-f5 all hit the same shape-mismatch root cause but each captures a DIFFERENT facet — confirmed by reading parts.ts:36-65 and ChatPane.tsx:88-130. Reality is THREE shapes, not two: (a) live streaming AI SDK part = type 'tool-<name>' with input/output/state; (b) persisted DB row in aegis_chat_messages.metadata.parts = type 'tool-call' with toolName + args (parts.ts:41-46) AND a separate type 'tool-result' part (parts.ts:54-60); (c) rehydrated UIMessage going to MessageBubble = type 'dynamic-tool' with toolName + input/output (ChatPane.tsx:107-115). Plan's deriveTodos is written against shape (b) only. Strip will be invisible during streaming AND on rehydration — only the persisted-then-reloaded path through buildInitialMessages matches, but that path REWRITES into shape (c) before MessageBubble or any sibling component sees it. Net: deriveTodos as written matches NONE of the three runtime shapes. Skeptic-f3 named the problem; aegis-fit-f5 caught the live/persisted divergence; test-strategy-f1/f2 spotted the args-vs-input field name and the missing round-trip test. Converge: the plan needs a single normalizer (mirror MessageBubble's isToolPart + toolNameFor + 'args ?? input') exported from a shared aegis-parts.ts, and a round-trip integration test that drives shapes (a)/(b)/(c) through it.
agreements.patch: Single shared normalizer in frontend/src/lib/aegis-parts.ts: extract toolName via 'part.toolName ?? (typeof part.type === string && part.type.startsWith(tool-) ? part.type.slice(5) : null)' AND extract args via 'part.args ?? part.input ?? {}'. Both deriveTodos AND MessageBubble's existing isToolPart/toolNameFor consume this helper. Add round-trip test: stream simulated tool-set_todos part -> stepsToMessageParts -> save -> buildInitialMessages -> deriveTodos -> assert Todo[]. ALSO add live-streaming fixture (raw tool-<name> shape, input/state but no persisted args yet) and assert deriveTodos still returns the array.

agreements.convergence: VERIFIED by reading agent.ts:87-108. onStepFinish unconditionally iterates toolCalls and calls saveToolExecution with hardcoded 'read_only'/'safe' for every call — no allowlist, no opt-out. Plan's 'NOT touching aegis_tool_executions' claim is factually false. A 12-todo turn writes 1 + 12 + 12 = 25 telemetry rows for pure UI bookkeeping, polluting tool-execution dashboards/spending counters. This is a P0-flavored 'side effect the plan denies exists'.
agreements.patch: Either (a) add CHAT_ONLY_TOOLS = new Set(['set_todos','update_todo']) check at agent.ts:93 that skips saveToolExecution, or (b) add optional `audit?: false` flag on AegisToolEntry and respect it in onStepFinish. Prefer (b) — extensible to future ephemeral UI tools. Plan must also drop the 'NOT touching aegis_tool_executions' bullet and call out the agent.ts edit explicitly in 'Files this feature will touch'.

agreements.convergence: VERIFIED at agent.ts:77 stopWhen: stepCountIs(25). Math holds: maxItems:12 with one set + per-item in_progress + per-item done = 25 calls EXACTLY at the cap, leaving zero steps for actual fix work. Even at maxItems:6 it's 13 telemetry calls vs 12 real-work steps. The plan's two-tool design fundamentally fights the agent's step budget. Skeptic-f4 hits the same problem from a $-cost angle (every update_todo is a full LLM round-trip). Pragmatist-f1 and aegis-fit-f9 propose the same convergent fix (collapse to one tool with full-list-replace OR batch update_todos plural).
agreements.patch: Combine two converging signals: (1) Collapse to single set_todos tool taking the FULL list every call (Cursor + Claude Code TodoWrite pattern, pragmatist-f1). One tool call per state transition instead of two. Cuts 12-todo turn from 25 calls to ~7. (2) Bump stopWhen to stepCountIs(40) as belt-and-suspenders. Lock maxItems:8 so worst-case is set_todos*N where N=number-of-state-transitions, well under cap.

agreements.convergence: VERIFIED by reading MessageBubble.tsx:99-153. parts.forEach pushes to elements/toolBuffer; flushTools() at end drains buffer. If every part is set_todos/update_todo (early-returned), elements stays empty AND toolBuffer stays empty AND text is ''. Yet the wrapping div at 147-153 still renders unconditionally with px-4 py-2 padding plus inner div with mx-auto max-w-3xl space-y-2 — producing a visible empty bubble (real estate eaten by padding alone). For multi-revise turns where the model emits ONLY set_todos+update_todos in one assistant message and reuses an earlier message for the prose summary, this lands. Test-strategy-auditor-f6 nominally covered MessageBubble regression but didn't anticipate the empty-render edge case.
agreements.patch: Add explicit guard at the end of MessageBubble: 'if (!isUser && !error && elements.length === 0) return null'. Add unit fixture in new message-bubble.test.tsx with parts=[set_todos, update_todo, update_todo] only — assert no DOM emitted (or only a fragment). This pairs with the suppression in Task 7.

dissents.reason: Skeptic-f6 frames the chat-strip vs FixPanel duplication as 'two near-identical lists'. From a system-coherence lens this is overstated. FixPanel renders a per-fix internal checklist (To-dos / Verification cards inside ONE plan); the chat strip renders the assistant TURN's orchestration plan ('revise plan A, then revise plan B') — different unit-of-work. The risk is real but it's a copy/onboarding axis (where do users look for status) not a structural duplication. Hard 'hide one when the other is visible' rule (skeptic-f6 patch) overcorrects and breaks the canonical revise-both-plans flow where BOTH are useful (chat strip = which plan we're on; FixPanel = inside the open plan). Keep architect-f8's lighter ask: grep-verify the FixPanel.tsx:372-432 citation, and add an explicit 'Surface boundaries' subsection clarifying the distinction without coupling the two surfaces.

ux-walker-f3 wants per-row 'mark done/cancelled manually' affordances in v1. This contradicts the plan's stated v1 simplification ('AI-managed only, no user editing — Cursor v1 was the same') and adds a state-management vector (client-only mutation that's not in the message-parts source of truth). Manual recovery from stuck state is real but ux-walker-f1's lighter 'Dismiss/clear strip when stream ended + non-terminal' is sufficient — it's purely a visibility flip, no source-of-truth divergence. Per-row manual editing is a v1.1 ask once we have data on how often agent abandons todos.

new_findings.claim: Independent of aegis-fit-f4 (which I now agree with), there is a SECOND consequence of suppressing set_todos/update_todo at the part level: if the agent emits a turn where parts = [set_todos, update_todo, request_fix(error)], the request_fix error path in MessageBubble.tsx:113 falls through to the gray pill via toolBuffer.push at line 137. But the fixId guard at line 119 means a request_fix error WITH a set_todos preceding it now produces a different element layout than today. Plan does not specify the interaction between MessageBubble's existing per-tool branches and the new suppression rule.
new_findings.evidence_or_alternative: MessageBubble.tsx:113-143 — request_fix error path uses early 'if ((toolName === request_fix || toolName === revise_fix) && !isError)' guard, so an error falls through to toolBuffer.push at line 137. Adding suppression for set_todos/update_todo BEFORE that branch is the only safe ordering.
new_findings.suggested_patch: Specify in Task 7: 'Add suppression IMMEDIATELY after toolName is computed (between line 106 and 113), BEFORE the request_fix/revise_fix branch. Use forEach return (skips iteration). Add explicit unit fixture for parts=[set_todos, update_todo, request_fix(error)] asserting the gray-pill error renders correctly.'

new_findings.plan_section: Data Model > Derivation rule (frontend) + Aegis-fit-f10 message history convergence
new_findings.claim: Aegis-fit-f10 is correct but understated: when AI SDK rebuilds prior-turn ModelMessages from UIMessages on the next user turn, it serializes tool-call parts (with args/input) AND tool-result parts back into the prompt. With two tools and 12 todos that's 25 tool-call+result pairs PER previous turn replayed in context. After 3 multi-step turns, that's 75 tool-call pairs in context just for UI bookkeeping — measurable token bloat. Skeptic-f7 hit this from the prompt-history-replay angle; aegis-fit-f10 hit it from the model-confusion angle. Same root cause: ephemeral UI state shouldn't round-trip through the model's context.
new_findings.evidence_or_alternative: saveAssistantMessage at agent.ts:137-142 persists parts (which include set_todos/update_todo). Future turns rebuild ModelMessages from these. Combined with skeptic-f7's prompt-balloon analysis and aegis-fit-f10's history-confusion analysis, this is structural.
new_findings.suggested_patch: On the backend message-rebuild path that converts prior-turn parts back to ModelMessages, filter out set_todos/update_todo tool-call/tool-result pairs (or compress to a single system-style note 'previous turn declared and completed N todos'). Plan must specify WHICH conversion path: this is in the route's stream handler, not buildInitialMessages. Grep backend/src/routes/aegis-v3-stream.ts (or equivalent) for ModelMessage construction and add the filter there. Add backend test asserting prior-turn set_todos parts do not appear in ModelMessages sent to the LLM.

new_findings.axis: tool-call without paired tool-result skipped on rehydration
new_findings.claim: parts.ts:48 only emits a UIMessage dynamic-tool part when there's a paired tool-result. Bare tool-call parts (mid-stream, abort, server crash before execute returns) are silently dropped on rehydration. Plan asserts 'page refresh restores state' as a Success Criterion — but a refresh DURING streaming, before set_todos's {ok:true} ack persists, will return zero todos.
new_findings.evidence_or_alternative: ChatPane.tsx:99-117 confirms: rawParts entries with type tool-call are skipped (the matching tool-result drives emission). For ephemeral UI state where the args ARE the state, this is wrong — set_todos's args should rehydrate even without a paired result.
new_findings.suggested_patch: Either (a) add a special-case in buildInitialMessages: if tool-call.toolName in {set_todos, update_todo} and no paired tool-result exists, still emit a dynamic-tool part with state input-available. Or (b) accept the limitation and document explicitly in plan: 'page refresh during streaming may show empty strip until next assistant message — only fully-acked tool calls rehydrate.' Pick (a) for v1 — it's 4 lines and matches Success Criterion #2.

revisions.reason: Still P3, unchanged. AegisToolContext shape verified at tool-types.ts; cross-call validation still infeasible in v1. The 'optionally validate' fork should remain stricken — drop the conditional language from API Design entirely.
